req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 4
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-095 defines three testable rules: (1) a manager may schedule a review >= 7 days in advance (positive EP), (2) dates < 7 days ahead must be rejected with 422 (negative EP/NEG), and (3) the exact 7-day boundary must be accepted while 6-day+23h59m boundary must be rejected (BVA). Rate-limiting adds a fourth case (NEG/DT) where exceeding the per-user quota returns 429. The five tests cover EP positive, NEG sub-7-day rejection, BVA at exactly now+7d (accepted), BVA at now+7d-1s (rejected), and DT rate-limit exhaustion.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_future_date
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
Reviewee user exists with id from <reviewee_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = utcnow + 14 days (well inside valid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token and scheduled_at
expected: Notification records created for manager and reviewee
trace: EP: date 14d ahead is in the valid equivalence class (>= now+7d)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_future_date(manager_auth_fixture, reviewee_fixture, utcnow_fn, api_client):
    token = manager_auth_fixture("manager_01")
    reviewee_id = reviewee_fixture("reviewee_01")
    scheduled_at = (utcnow_fn() + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat() + "Z"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["reviewee_id"] == reviewee_id
    notifications = api_client.get(
        f"/notifications?review_id={body['id']}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    ).json()
    notified_ids = {n["user_id"] for n in notifications}
    assert reviewee_id in notified_ids

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
Reviewee user exists with id from <reviewee_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = utcnow - 1 day (clearly invalid partition: past date)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token and scheduled_at
expected: Response body contains error referencing scheduled_at constraint
trace: R-WEB-095: past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-095: error pathway 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
NEG: past date is deep inside the invalid equivalence class
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected(manager_auth_fixture, reviewee_fixture, utcnow_fn, api_client):
    token = manager_auth_fixture("manager_01")
    reviewee_id = reviewee_fixture("reviewee_01")
    scheduled_at = (utcnow_fn() - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat() + "Z"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
Reviewee user exists with id from <reviewee_fixture>
System clock accessible via <utcnow_fn> returning a stable frozen time
actions: Compute scheduled_at = utcnow + exactly 7 days (on-boundary valid value)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token and scheduled_at
expected: Response body contains review id confirming acceptance at the boundary
trace: R-WEB-095: at least 7 days in advance — boundary is inclusive
BVA: now+7d is the minimum valid boundary point
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timedelta

def test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_accepted(manager_auth_fixture, reviewee_fixture, utcnow_fn, api_client):
    token = manager_auth_fixture("manager_01")
    reviewee_id = reviewee_fixture("reviewee_01")
    scheduled_at = (utcnow_fn() + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat() + "Z"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert "id" in resp.json()

tests.name: test_schedule_review_sub_7_days_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
Reviewee user exists with id from <reviewee_fixture>
System clock accessible via <utcnow_fn> returning a stable frozen time
actions: Compute scheduled_at = utcnow + 7 days - 1 second (just below boundary)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token and scheduled_at
expected: Response error references scheduled_at minimum constraint
trace: R-WEB-095: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-095: error pathway 422 when scheduled_at < now+7d
BVA: now+7d-1s is the first invalid boundary point immediately below minimum
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timedelta

def test_schedule_review_sub_7_days_boundary_rejected(manager_auth_fixture, reviewee_fixture, utcnow_fn, api_client):
    token = manager_auth_fixture("manager_01")
    reviewee_id = reviewee_fixture("reviewee_01")
    scheduled_at = (utcnow_fn() + timedelta(days=7) - timedelta(seconds=1)).isoformat() + "Z"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_schedule_review_rate_limit_enforced
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
Reviewee pool available via <reviewee_pool_fixture> (distinct ids to avoid duplicate-review conflicts)
Rate-limit quota for the manager role known via <rate_limit_config> (e.g. 5 per minute)
actions: Send N requests equal to the known per-user rate limit quota, each with a valid date (now+14d)
Send one additional (N+1) request with a valid date
trace: R-WEB-095: operation must be rate-limited per user
DT: decision row — quota exhausted AND valid payload => 429 override
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import timedelta

def test_schedule_review_rate_limit_enforced(
    manager_auth_fixture, reviewee_pool_fixture, rate_limit_config, utcnow_fn, api_client
):
    token = manager_auth_fixture("manager_rate_test")
    quota = rate_limit_config["schedule_review_per_user"]
    reviewees = reviewee_pool_fixture(quota + 1)
    scheduled_at = (utcnow_fn() + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat() + "Z"
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    for i in range(quota):
        r = api_client.post(
            "/performance-reviews/schedule",
            json={"reviewee_id": reviewees[i], "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
            headers=headers,
        )
        assert r.status_code == 201, f"Request {i+1} failed unexpectedly: {r.status_code}"

    overflow = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json={"reviewee_id": reviewees[quota], "scheduled_at": scheduled_at},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert overflow.status_code == 429
    assert "retry-after" in overflow.headers
